espresso.headline: OpenAI ships o3-mini with 3× faster inference and full tool-calling support
espresso.body: OpenAI quietly released o3-mini to API users overnight, cutting median response latency from 4.2s to 1.4s on reasoning benchmarks while maintaining 94% of o3's accuracy on MATH and HumanEval. The model now supports parallel tool-calling and structured JSON output natively, removing the need for prompt-level workarounds. This is the first o-series model priced below GPT-4o, making frontier-level reasoning economically viable for high-volume production workloads.

cold_brew.title: Mixture of Experts (MoE) — How modern LLMs scale without scaling compute
cold_brew.summary: Most large language models activate every single parameter for every single token — which gets expensive fast. Mixture of Experts sidesteps this by splitting the model into specialised sub-networks called 'experts', and using a lightweight router to activate only 2–4 of them per token. The result is a model with the parameter count of a 100B+ model but the inference cost of a 7B one. GPT-4, Mixtral 8x7B, and Gemini 1.5 are all believed to use MoE internally.
takeaways: Only a fraction of parameters fire per token — massive compute savings at inference time
The router is a small learned network; getting routing right is the main research challenge
MoE models can be unstable to train — load balancing across experts requires careful loss design
Mixtral 8x7B is open-source and a good starting point if you want to study MoE hands-on

pastry.title: Mastra — TypeScript-native AI agent framework by the Gatsby team
pastry.why_it_matters: Mastra lets you define agents, tools, and multi-step workflows in plain TypeScript with full type safety — no YAML configs, no Python context switching. It ships with built-in memory, tool-calling, and a local dev UI, making it the most ergonomic option for Node.js teams who want to add agentic features without leaving their existing stack.
